description: The figure presents a dynamic recrystallization (DRX) state diagram for Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density high-strength steel under hot deformation conditions, plotting the natural logarithm of the Zener-Hollomon parameter (lnZ) on the vertical axis against true strain (ε) on the horizontal axis. The diagram features two distinct datasets: open circles represent the critical strain (εc) marking the onset of DRX, while red stars denote the steady-state strain (εs) corresponding to complete DRX. The plot is partitioned into three characteristic deformation regimes: Area A (work hardening zone), Area B (partial DRX zone), and Area C (complete DRX zone). The data demonstrates that both εc and εs increase monotonically with increasing Z parameter (associated with decreasing temperature or increasing strain rate), indicating that DRX initiation and completion become progressively more difficult under higher lnZ conditions. This state diagram establishes quantitative boundaries for processing window selection, confirming that higher deformation resistance (higher Z) requires greater accumulated strain to achieve complete dynamic recrystallization.
